meaning: The sole constant used alongside eml to generate all elementary functions
RPN string encoding of the natural logarithm ln x in pure EML form
Number of parameters in the level-n master formula
Glaisher-Kinkelin constant (~1.28243), used as a transcendental test value for y
Example binary function B(x,y) = x - y/2 used to illustrate traps in Sheffer operator analysis
Initial set of constants, functions, and operators to be reconstructed (defines 'computing all elementary functions')
Remaining target operator set at iteration i of the bootstrapping procedure
3-primitive calculator configuration using exp and binary logarithm with e absorbed into exp
4-primitive calculator configuration using binary exponentiation and binary logarithm with terminal constant e or π
4-primitive calculator configuration using exp, ln, and subtraction
6-primitive calculator configuration using exp, ln, negation, reciprocal, and addition, accepting any real constant as input
Lean 4 proof assistant's default junk value for the complex logarithm at zero
Shorthand notation for the eml operation in RPN strings
A close variant of EML defined by equation (4b)
The calculator button corresponding to the eml operation
A Sheffer operator discovered by the author, central to the paper's contributions
The level-2 master formula as a function of input x parameterized by alpha_i, beta_i, gamma_i
Kolmogorov-style complexity, measured as the length of a Reverse Polish Notation (RPN) calculator program
Non-terminal symbol in the EML context-free grammar representing any elementary function expression
The grammar rule generating all EML expression trees using only the constant 1 and the eml operator
Initial set of constants, functions, and operators verified as known/available
Known operator set at iteration i of the bootstrapping procedure
Ternary operator candidate defined as e^(x/ln x) times ln z / e^y, satisfying T(x,x,x)=1
Procedure that verifies whether a given operator set can reconstruct all target primitives
Constant coefficient in the linear combination input representation
General linear combination representing each input to eml in the master formula, unifying constant 1, variable x, and previous eml output f
Arithmetic mean (x+y)/2
Coefficient of input variable x in the linear combination input representation
The EDL cousin operator defined as exp(x) / ln(y), with constant e
Euler's formula: exp(iφ) = cos(φ) + i·sin(φ)
Context-free grammar production rule representing the binary EML Sheffer operation applied to two sub-expressions
The binary operator defined as exp(x) - ln(y), called the Exp-Minus-Log operator
The -EML cousin operator defined as ln(x) - exp(y), with constant negative infinity
Exponential function e^x
First (counterclockwise) input to the EML Sheffer operator, representing the argument of the exponential component
Result from a previous eml computation used as input to higher-level eml nodes
Coefficient of previous eml result f in the linear combination input representation
Hypotenuse function sqrt(x^2+y^2)
Imaginary unit, square root of -1
Reciprocal function 1/x
Natural logarithm
Extended real arithmetic identities required for correct evaluation of EML-compiled expressions
Second input to the EML Sheffer operator, representing the argument of the logarithm component
Arbitrary base x logarithm of y
Sign flip unary function -x
Exponentiation x^y
Predecessor function: x - 1
Square function x^2
Successor function: x + 1
Euler-Mascheroni constant (~0.577216), used as a transcendental test value for x
Logistic sigmoid function 1/(1+e^{-x})
